mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through lower cost of capital for corporate cash flows.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.0% as of 2008-08-01 indicates easing cycle
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following credit stress
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100,000 persons in one month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137,473 thousand as of 2008-08-01 in declining trend
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances following PAYEMS declines > 100k, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below 900,000 annualized units signals weakening residential investment demand and reduced construction employment multiplier effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through reduced economic growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 965,000 units in 2008-07-01 vintage, declining
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when HOUST < 900k, or Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and 20-day forward returns exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production index below 110 signals contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization and real economic activity, reducing corporate profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days through immediate earnings revision expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances when INDPRO < 110, or rolling 1-year Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based demand weakness across consumer and investment channels. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, the multiplier effect on equity valuations is amplified, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 50,000 units month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Both RSAFS and HOUST showing weakness in 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression of direction_20d on RSAFS and HOUST interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6.0% combined with declining payroll employment signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through consumption channel.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.0% AND PAYEMS declines > 50,000 persons in same month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1% and PAYEMS declining as of 2008-08-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the joint effect of UNRATE and PAYEMS is not significantly more predictive than either series alone (nested F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2.5% and inflation (CPI) is rising above 3.0% annualized, real monetary policy becomes restrictive despite nominal easing. This regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the Fed faces stagflationary constraints on further accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.0% and CPIAUCSL at 219.181 in 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of stagflationary regime instances, or the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL momentum is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) in a declining trend over two consecutive quarters signals recession entry. When quarterly GDP growth turns negative, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days become negative as equity valuations reprice for lower long-term earnings growth and increased recession risk.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate falls below 0.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,740.3 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2008-04-01 vintage in early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 0.5% annualized, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
